title: [Foreman substrate-capture] Agent-tool CLI flag-drift recurring friction — 4+ instances across 2 agents this window; pending-graduations candidate proposed
body:
Foreman substrate-capture (small-job pickup): agent-tool CLI flag-drift recurring friction across this session window. Picking up Twilit ST's named-but-unowned observation from event `[Twilit heartbeat ~16:10Z]` per owner direction "look for any orphaned changes or other session by-products or accumulating jobs and sort them out".

## Observation

The `pnpm agent-tools:collaboration-state -- <subcommand>` CLI surface produces repeated flag-mismatch errors in active use. Each requires reading `--help` output + retrying. The cost compounds over a multi-agent session.

## Instances this window (≥4 across 2 agents)

| Agent | Subcommand | Attempted flag | Actual valid flag | Source |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| Scorched (me) | `comms send` | `--kind narrative --tag team-member` | (no `--kind`; no `--tag`; `--platform` + `--model` required) | First takeover broadcast retry |
| Twilit ST | `comms direct` | `--created-at <iso>` | `--now <iso>` only | Per Twilit's chat-summary observation |
| Twilit ST | `comms direct` | `--title <subject>` (prior session memory) | (different flag in current CLI) | Per Twilit's chat-summary observation |
| Twilit ST | `claims close` | `--closed <timestamp>` | `--closed <path>` (it's a path, not timestamp) | Per Twilit's chat-summary observation |

## Pattern shape

- The CLI surface has **stable subcommand names** but **flag names drift between subcommands** (e.g., `--now` for `direct` but maybe `--now` is also `comms send`'s timestamp flag; flags carry different semantics across subcommands).
- Mental models from prior-session experience or peer comms (e.g., "I saw Foamy use `--tag` somewhere") create **stale flag-expectations** that compile into immediate error on retry-required retries.
- The error message is helpful (prints full subcommand help) — friction is reading-and-retry-loop, not silent failure.

## Adjacency to existing graduated rules

- `feedback_commit_format_recurring_friction` (commit-format ergonomics) — same shape: CLI-surface ergonomics produce per-session friction; cure is the platform-agnostic commit skill (owner-directed work item).
- `feedback_use_built_agent_tools_only` (graduated to `.agent/rules/use-built-agent-tools-cli.md`) — rule constrains agents to use the built CLI; rule does NOT address CLI ergonomics inside that constraint.
- `feedback_agent_tool_help_on_invalid_flags` (graduated to `agent-tools/README.md` §"CLI Norms") — already addresses the friction-on-error path; help output IS being printed; that's not the gap.

## Diagnosis (Foreman hypothesis)

The graduated CLI norm requires help on invalid flag — **the immediate friction IS the norm working**. The structural friction is one layer up: the **mental model of which flag-name applies to which subcommand**. This is a documentation / discoverability gap, not a CLI-norm gap.

Candidate cures (Director routes whoever picks this up):

1. **CLI cheat-sheet substrate** — `agent-tools/docs/CHEAT_SHEET.md` or similar, one-row-per-subcommand quick-ref with required flags + common gotchas. Lower context-load than reading `--help` per subcommand.
2. **AGENT.md cross-reference** — short pointer from `AGENT.md` index path to the cheat-sheet, so agents discover it at session-open rather than at flag-error-time.
3. **Convergence cure** — flag-name normalization across subcommands (e.g., `--now` always means "ISO timestamp"; `--platform` + `--model` always required for identity-emitting subcommands). Higher engineering cost; durable cure.
4. **In-shell completion** — `pnpm` doesn't easily plumb shell-completion through to the underlying CLI; expensive cure.

## Pending-graduations candidate (proposal to next consolidator)

Add entry to `.agent/memory/operational/pending-graduations.md`:

```markdown
- **Agent-tool CLI flag-name discoverability gap**: agents repeatedly hit flag-mismatch errors (≥4 instances in single 2026-05-23 window; Scorched + Twilit ST). Help-on-error norm is functioning; gap is one layer up at mental-model formation. Candidate cure: `agent-tools/docs/CHEAT_SHEET.md` (lightweight quick-ref) cross-referenced from `AGENT.md` index. Trigger: second consecutive multi-agent session with ≥3 flag-mismatch instances OR owner direction to invest in CLI ergonomics. Status: pending second-instance evidence.
```

## Foreman action taken

This substrate event. No file edits (avoid marshal-cycle interference). Next consolidator can fold into napkin / pending-graduations during a hygiene cycle.

## Tag

behaviour-note + accumulating-job-pickup.

— Scorched Tempering Kiln / claude / claude-opus-4-7 / 52b263 (Foreman, accumulated-job-sweep #1)
